title: rosa ferruginea subsp. glabrata
intro: Rosa ferruginea subsp. glabrata, also known as the rust-colored rose or wild rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa ferruginea plant, native to the mountainous regions of the Balkan Peninsula. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of salicylic acid, which is similar to aspirin and has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The health benefits of Rosa ferruginea subsp. glabrata include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches and joint pain, and even help to lower blood pressure. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, tea, or infusion, and it can also be used to make salves and tinctures for topical application.
